senses.definition: To trust or have faith in someone or something (now archaic).
examples: He dared not affy his own judgment in such a grave matter.
They would not affy the safety of the town to a stranger.

senses.definition: To promise or pledge, especially to marry (now archaic).
examples: He was long affied to her before the wedding day.
Their families affied the young couple in a quiet ceremony.

senses.definition: A signed statement (affidavit) that a contest winner completes to prove they are allowed to receive a prize (US slang).
examples: The sweepstakes company sent her an affy to sign before sending the check.
Winners must return the affy within ten days or forfeit the prize.

senses.definition: A humorous or non-standard spelling of affidavit, a written statement that is signed and sworn to be true.
examples: He swore on an affydavy that he had never seen the man before.
The old letter jokingly calls the legal paper an affydavy.

senses.definition: To pledge or bind by promise, especially to marriage (obsolete).
examples: The knight affyded his heart to her in solemn words.
They were affyded one to another by their families.

senses.definition: Present participle of affy: trusting or pledging oneself (now archaic).
examples: Affying in his friends, he told them all his fears.
She stood before the altar, affying herself to him for life.

senses.definition: A person from Afghanistan or whose family comes from Afghanistan.
examples: Many Afghans now live and work in cities around the world.
She met an Afghan who had recently moved to London.

senses.definition: Relating to Afghanistan, its people, or its culture.
examples: They opened a restaurant that serves Afghan food.
The museum has a collection of Afghan carpets.

senses.definition: A thick, often colorful blanket or shawl, usually knitted or crocheted.
examples: She sat on the sofa with an afghan over her knees.
Her grandmother made a large afghan for the bed.

senses.definition: A tall, elegant breed of dog with long, fine hair and a narrow head.
examples: Their Afghan won first prize at the dog show.
She brushed her Afghan every day to keep its coat smooth.

senses.definition: Small Afghan-style blankets, scarves, or decorative pieces, especially ones that are knitted or crocheted.
examples: She sold tiny afghanets for dolls at the craft fair.
The table was covered with several bright afghanets.

senses.definition: The money used in Afghanistan.
examples: She exchanged her dollars for Afghanis at the airport.
The price of bread was ten Afghanis.

senses.definition: Relating to the currency of Afghanistan.
examples: The store listed prices in both dollars and Afghani units.
He checked the current Afghani exchange rate.

senses.definition: Plural of Afghani, referring to units of Afghanistan's currency.
examples: The meal cost about 500 Afghanis.
He saved several thousand Afghanis for his trip.

senses.definition: A country in South and Central Asia whose capital city is Kabul.
examples: She worked in Afghanistan for two years as a teacher.
Afghanistan shares borders with several countries in Asia.

senses.definition: Plural of Afghan, meaning people from Afghanistan.
examples: Many Afghans have moved abroad in recent decades.
The project was designed in close partnership with Afghans.

senses.definition: Plural of Afghan, meaning blankets or shawls that are knitted or crocheted.
examples: She keeps several afghans on the sofa for guests.
The shop sells handmade afghans in many colors.

senses.definition: Plural of Afghan, meaning Afghan hounds.
examples: The breeder showed three Afghans at the competition.
Afghans require regular grooming because of their long coats.

senses.definition: An idol or false god, especially one that people worship instead of the true God (archaic spelling).
examples: They warned the people not to bow to any afgod.
In the old text, the prophet speaks against every afgod of the land.

senses.definition: A rare medical condition in which a person has almost no fibrinogen, a protein needed for blood to clot normally.
examples: Patients with afibrinogenemia may suffer from severe bleeding even after small injuries.
Genetic testing can confirm a diagnosis of afibrinogenemia.

senses.definition: A woman who likes something very much and knows a lot about it.
examples: She is a real jazz aficionada.
As a coffee aficionada, she travels the world to find rare beans.

senses.definition: Plural of aficionada, meaning women who are very interested in and know a lot about something.
examples: Wine aficionadas gathered for the special tasting.
The club brings together literature aficionadas from across the city.

senses.definition: Someone who likes and knows a lot about a particular activity, subject, or sport.
examples: He is a film aficionado who watches several movies a week.
As a football aficionado, he never misses a match.

senses.definition: Plural of aficionado, meaning people who like and know a lot about a particular activity, subject, or sport.
examples: Wine aficionados traveled from all over the country for the festival.
The show is popular with jazz aficionados.

senses.definition: Far away from the place where you usually are, or away from home.
examples: Many young people now work far afield from their home towns.
The team travelled afield in search of new customers.

senses.definition: Away from the main subject or purpose of something.
examples: The discussion wandered afield and soon lost focus.
Her research never strays far afield from this central question.

senses.definition: A family name or personal name of Arabic origin.
examples: Dr. Afifi gave a lecture on Islamic art.
She published her first novel under the name Afifi.

senses.definition: A piece of matzah that is hidden and later eaten at the end of the Jewish Passover meal.
examples: The children ran around the house looking for the afikomen.
Traditionally, the person who finds the afikomen receives a small gift.

senses.definition: Burning or on fire.
examples: The dry grass was soon afire.
Within minutes, the whole barn was afire.

senses.definition: Full of strong emotion, such as excitement or passion.
examples: Her imagination was afire with new ideas.
The crowd was afire with hope after the speech.

senses.definition: Not having a flagellum or flagella, especially in reference to cells or microorganisms.
examples: Some aflagellar bacteria rely on other methods of movement.
The species has both flagellated and aflagellar forms.

senses.definition: Traditional underground water channels used for irrigation in parts of the Middle East, especially Oman.
examples: The village still depends on the ancient aflaj for its water supply.
Engineers studied the aflaj system to understand sustainable irrigation methods.

senses.definition: Burning with flames.
examples: By the time the fire crew arrived, the building was aflame.
The dry forest was soon aflame after the lightning strike.

senses.definition: Full of strong feeling or intense activity.
examples: The city was aflame with protest.
Her cheeks were aflame with embarrassment.

senses.definition: Shining or burning with a bright, unsteady light.
examples: Torches were aflare along the walls.
The campfire was still aflare in the early morning.

senses.definition: Completely flat or level (rare or dialect).
examples: The land there lies aflat for miles.
He pressed the cloth aflat against the table.

senses.definition: A poisonous substance made by certain molds that can grow on stored foods such as peanuts and corn, and that can cause cancer.
examples: Scientists monitor crops carefully to prevent aflatoxin contamination.
High levels of aflatoxin in food can be dangerous for human health.

senses.definition: A group of poisonous substances made by certain molds that can grow on stored crops and foods.
examples: Aflatoxins are a major concern in warm, humid regions.
Regulations limit the amount of aflatoxins allowed in food products.

senses.definition: A sudden, often powerful feeling of inspiration, especially believed to come from a divine source.
examples: He claimed that the poem came to him in a single aflatus of creativity.
The speech seemed to be driven by some mysterious aflatus.

senses.definition: Openly showing or waving, especially in a proud or showy way (obsolete or dialect).
examples: Their banners flew aflaunt in the wind.
He rode by with his colors aflaunt.

senses.definition: On one side or sideways (dialect or obsolete).
examples: The gate hung afley on its broken hinges.
His cap sat afley on his head.

senses.definition: Flashing or shining with a quick, unsteady light.
examples: Lights were aflicker in the distance.
The candles stood aflicker in the cold wind.

senses.definition: In flight; flying or up in the air.
examples: A flock of birds was aflight over the valley.
Arrows were soon aflight from the castle walls.

senses.definition: Floating on the surface of water.
examples: The small boat managed to stay afloat in the storm.
Several pieces of wood were still afloat after the ship sank.

senses.definition: Managing to survive, especially financially.
examples: The company is just staying afloat in a difficult market.
They took a loan to keep their business afloat.

senses.definition: Flowing or moving like a stream.
examples: Tears were aflow down her cheeks.
The river was aflow after the heavy rains.

senses.definition: Covered with flowers or in full bloom.
examples: By late spring, the whole garden was aflower.
The hillside lay aflower with wild blossoms.

senses.definition: Playing or winning by luck, rather than by skill (informal, rare).
examples: He accused his opponent of afluking his way through the tournament.
They thought the team was just afluking it this season.

senses.definition: Flushed or red in the face.
examples: Her cheeks were aflush with embarrassment.
He came in, aflush from running up the stairs.

senses.definition: Even with or level with something.
examples: The deck was built aflush with the back door.
Tiles were laid aflush with the floorboards.

senses.definition: Moving or shaking with quick, light movements, like wings.
examples: The birds were aflutter in the trees.
Her dress was aflutter in the wind.

senses.definition: Very excited or nervous.
examples: She was all aflutter before her first performance.
The town was aflutter with rumors.

senses.definition: Covered with foam or producing foam.
examples: The sea was afoam after the storm.
The horses came back afoam and tired.

senses.definition: Having no focal point, especially in optical systems.
examples: The microscope uses an afocal attachment for certain observations.
Afocal systems are useful in some types of telescopes.

senses.definition: On foot; walking rather than using a vehicle.
examples: They set out afoot just after sunrise.
Most of the journey was made afoot through the forest.

senses.definition: Happening or being prepared now.
examples: There are big changes afoot in the company.
A new plan is afoot to improve the service.

senses.definition: Before (in time or in a written text) (archaic or formal).
examples: As aforesaid, the parties agree to the terms set out afore.
The reasons given afore shall not be repeated here.

senses.definition: Before now; earlier (archaic).
examples: I have never seen such a thing afore.
As I told you afore, we have little time.

senses.definition: Mentioned or written about earlier in the same document (archaic or legal).
examples: The parties agree to the aforegoing terms and conditions.
Subject to the aforegoing provisions, the contract shall remain in force.

senses.definition: Before something happens; in advance (now mainly dialect or archaic).
examples: You should have told me aforehand if you were coming.
We agreed the price aforehand.

senses.definition: Mentioned earlier in the same piece of writing or speech.
examples: The aforementioned issues will be discussed in detail below.
Please refer to the aforementioned report for more information.

senses.definition: Named earlier in the same document (mainly legal).
examples: The aforenamed parties agree to the terms set out below.
Payment shall be made by the aforenamed company.

senses.definition: Mentioned earlier in the same document or statement, especially in legal writing.
examples: The aforesaid sum shall be paid within thirty days.
The tenant agrees to comply with the aforesaid conditions.

senses.definition: Planned or thought about before something happens, especially before doing something bad.
examples: It was not a crime of aforethought but a sudden reaction.
The act showed clear aforethought and careful planning.

senses.definition: Plans or ideas formed before an action, especially when they show intention.
examples: His aforethoughts revealed a careful strategy.
They examined her aforethoughts as recorded in the diary.

senses.definition: At some earlier time; in the past (archaic or literary).
examples: The castle had been rich and lively aforetime.
As aforetime, the bells rang out over the town.
